chapter_title: Sale or Transfer of Motor Vehicles & Master Keys
text:
(a) A person who violates Section 728.002 (Sale of Motor Vehicles on Consecutive Saturday and Sunday Prohibited) is subject to a civil penalty of:

(1) not more than $500 for a first violation;

(2) not less than $500 or more than $1,000 for a second violation; or

(3) not less than $1,000 or more than $5,000 for a third or subsequent violation.

(b) On a finding by the trier of fact that a person wilfully or with conscious indifference violated Section 728.002 (Sale of Motor Vehicles on Consecutive Saturday and Sunday Prohibited) , the court may triple the penalty due under Subsection (a).
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 728. Sale or Transfer of Motor Vehicles & Master Keys
##### § 728.003 — Civil Penalty

(a) A person who violates Section 728.002 (Sale of Motor Vehicles on Consecutive Saturday and Sunday Prohibited) is subject to a civil penalty of:

    (1) not more than $500 for a first violation;

    (2) not less than $500 or more than $1,000 for a second violation; or

    (3) not less than $1,000 or more than $5,000 for a third or subsequent violation.

(b) On a finding by the trier of fact that a person wilfully or with conscious indifference violated Section 728.002 (Sale of Motor Vehicles on Consecutive Saturday and Sunday Prohibited) , the court may triple the penalty due under Subsection (a).

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

not more than $500 for a first violation;
not less than $500 or more than $1,000 for a second violation; or
not less than $1,000 or more than $5,000 for a third or subsequent violation.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: On a finding by the trier of fact that a person wilfully or with conscious indifference violated Section 728.002 (Sale of Motor Vehicles on Consecutive Saturday and Sunday Prohibited) , the court may triple the penalty due under Subsection (a).
